title: Operator-precedence guard around conditional rendering
why: When loading is truthy, || short-circuits and the whole expression resolves to that truthy value instead of the guarded view(). The intended block is then only reachable through the second branch, so it silently never renders during the loading state. Unioning the booleans first with (loading || ready) and gating once with && restores the intended condition.
summary: Same precedence slip found in 4 places: 2 fixed, 1 open, 1 confirmed fine.

findings.explanation: During the initial fetch, loading is true, so the old expression returned true and the panel never rendered. Now both flags are unioned before the single guard.
Identical shape with pending/hasData. The header was missing for the entire pending window until the union fix.
Same precedence slip on busy/loaded. Fix proposed but not yet applied — the totals block does not render while busy is true.
Matched the grep but is not the bug: this is a deliberate either/or fallback, not a boolean guard around a conditional render. No change needed.
